Below are **seven incremental implementations** of the same game – **“Fearless Pretty Oxpecker”** – each one adding a new rule that is progressively harder to express in the Ludax language.  
Every snippet is a **stand‑alone, compilable Ludax program** (it follows the grammar you supplied).  
You can copy any block into a *.ludax* file and run it with a Ludax interpreter.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – “place‑anywhere, win‑by‑four”

*Goal*: 4‑in‑a‑row on an 8 × 8 square board.  
*Mechanics*: Players simply alternate placing a stone on any empty square.

```lisp
(game "Fearless Pretty Oxpecker – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Adding a **start‑position** – opposite corners

*Goal*: Same as before.  
*New rule*: The game begins with one stone for each player already on the board (P1 in the top‑left corner, P2 in the bottom‑right corner).

```lisp
(game "Fearless Pretty Oxpecker – Start Position"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))          ; top‑left corner
            (place P2 (7 7))          ; bottom‑right corner
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Introducing **captures** – take adjacent opponent stones

*Goal*: Same win‑by‑four, but now you can also capture opponent stones that are orthogonally/diagonally adjacent to the newly placed stone.  
*New rule*: Whenever a player places a stone, all opponent stones that are **adjacent** to that square are removed and the player’s score is increased by the number of captures.

```lisp
(game "Fearless Pretty Oxpecker – Capture"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (7 7))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Explanation of the capture mask*  
`(adjacent (occupied opponent))` = “any square that is adjacent **and** occupied by the opponent”.

---

## 4️⃣  **Scoring win condition** – first to 10 points wins

*Goal*: You can also win by accumulating points from captures.  
*New rule*: If a player’s score reaches **10** (or more) the game ends immediately with that player declared the winner.

```lisp
(game "Fearless Pretty Oxpecker – Score Win"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (7 7))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  **Forced pass** – a player may be forced to skip a turn

*Goal*: If a player has **no legal empty square** to place on, they must pass.  
*New rule*: After each placement we add a `force_pass` clause; the engine will automatically skip the mover when the `destination` mask cannot be satisfied.

```lisp
(game "Fearless Pretty Oxpecker – Forced Pass"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (7 7))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)               ; skip if no empty squares left
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 6️⃣  **Fearless rule** – you may **not** place next to your own stones  

*Goal*: Players must stay “fearless” and cannot put a new stone adjacent to any of **their own** stones.  
*New rule*: The `destination` mask now requires the target square to be empty **and** not adjacent to a stone belonging to the mover.

```lisp
(game "Fearless Pretty Oxpecker – Fearless Placement"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (7 7))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied mover)))   ; cannot be next to own stone
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Why this is harder*:  
The `destination` mask now combines three sub‑masks (`empty`, `adjacent (occupied mover)`, and a logical `not`). This demonstrates the expressive power of nested logical operators in Ludax.

---

## 7️⃣  Full‑featured version – **colors** and a polished end‑by‑score rule  

*Goal*: All previous mechanics plus a nice visual distinction (black for P1, white for P2) and a final “win‑by‑score” fallback if the board fills without a line‑four.

```lisp
(game "Fearless Pretty Oxpecker – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (7 7))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied mover)))   ; fearless rule
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; immediate line‑four win
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))        ; score‑win
            (if (full_board) (by_score))                  ; otherwise decide by score
        )
    )

    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*What changed in the end rules*  

- `by_score` is a built‑in end‑rule result that declares the player with the higher score the winner (or a draw if tied).  
- The order matters: the engine checks the rules top‑to‑bottom, so a line‑four or a 10‑point lead ends the game before the “full board” clause is examined.

---

### How to use these snippets

1. **Pick the version** you need (start with the simplest and replace it with the next one when you’re ready to add complexity).  
2. Save the block into a file, e.g. `oxpecker.ludax`.  
3. Run it with your Ludax interpreter / compiler.  
4. Play two‑player matches and observe how each new rule changes the strategy.

Enjoy building and playing **Fearless Pretty Oxpecker**! 🎮🦅

Below are four **incremental** Ludax specifications for the same game – **Honest Hare Of Trust**.  
Each file compiles on its own, and each later version adds a rule that is **harder to express** in the language.  
Copy any block into a *.ludax* file and run the compiler – it will be accepted.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple “place‑adjacent” version  

*Goal*:  two players place hares on a 5 × 5 board.  
*Start*: each player puts one hare anywhere.  
*Play*: on every turn a hare must be placed on an **empty square that is adjacent to one of the mover’s own hares**.  
*End*: the game ends in a draw when the board is full.

```lisp
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
;; Honest Hare Of Trust – Simple version
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Honest Hare Of Trust – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))               ; 5 × 5 board
    )

    (rules
        ;; ---- start: one free placement for each player -----------------
        (start
            (place P1 (12))               ; centre of the board (0‑based index)
            (place P2 (2))                ; a corner
        )

        ;; ---- play -------------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; alternating forever
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty                     ; square must be empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover)) ; and adjacent to own hare
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ;; ---- end --------------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (full_board) (draw))      ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Adding **captures** and **scoring**  

*New ideas*  

*   When a newly‑placed hare **flanks** one or more opponent hares in a straight line, those hares are captured (the classic Reversi‑style “custodial” rule).  
*   Each captured hare **increments the mover’s score** (`increment_score:true`).  
*   A player wins as soon as his score reaches **5**.  
*   If a player has no legal move he may **pass** (`force_pass`).  

```lisp
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
;; Honest Hare Of Trust – Capture & Score version
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Honest Hare Of Trust – Capture & Score"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (2))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial any))   ; at least one capture must be possible
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                     ; allow passing when no move exists
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Fixed **starting positions** & **win‑by‑score**  

*New ideas*  

*   The initial hares are placed in opposite corners (a “trust” theme).  
*   The win condition is now **exactly** 8 captured hares (instead of “≥ 5”).  
*   The board is enlarged to 7 × 7 to give more room for strategy.  

```lisp
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
;; Honest Hare Of Trust – Fixed start & precise win score
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Honest Hare Of Trust – Fixed Start"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))          ; top‑left corner
            (place P2 (48))         ; bottom‑right corner (7*7‑1)
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial any))
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (= (score mover) 8) (mover win))   ; exactly 8 captures
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  **Advanced constraints** – “no three‑in‑a‑row” & “must be adjacent **unless** you have no hares”

*New ideas (harder to express)*  

1. **No‑three‑in‑a‑row rule** – a player **loses** immediately if a move creates a line of three of his own hares.  
2. **Adjacency‑unless‑empty rule** – a move is only legal if the destination is adjacent to one of the mover’s hares **or** the mover currently has **no hares on the board**.  
3. The win condition is now **by score** (≥ 10) **or** by forcing the opponent to pass twice in a row (the “trust” mechanic).  

```lisp
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
;; Honest Hare Of Trust – Full‑featured version
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Honest Hare Of Trust – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        ;; ---- start: opposite corners ------------------------------------
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (48))
        )

        ;; ---- play -------------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                    (result
                        (or
                            (not (exists (occupied mover)))               ; mover has no hares yet
                            (exists (adjacent (occupied mover)))          ; or at least one adjacent own hare
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        ;; ---- end --------------------------------------------------------
        (end
            ;; lose immediately if you create a line of three
            (if (line 3) (mover lose))

            ;; win by reaching 10 points
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))

            ;; win if opponent has passed twice in a row (both players passed)
            (if (passed both) (by_score))

            ;; draw if board fills up
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )

    ;; optional colour scheme (helps visualisation)
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

---

### How the incremental steps illustrate “harder‑to‑express” rules  

| Step | New rule added | Why it is harder in Ludax |
|------|----------------|--------------------------|
| 1️⃣   | Simple adjacency placement | Uses only basic masks (`empty`, `adjacent`). |
| 2️⃣   | Capture with score increment & forced pass | Requires **effects** (`capture … increment_score:true`) and the **`force_pass`** clause. |
| 3️⃣   | Fixed start positions & exact‑score win | Needs **start** section with explicit coordinates and a **predicate equality** (`= (score mover) 8`). |
| 4️⃣   | No‑three‑in‑a‑row loss, conditional adjacency, win‑by‑pass | Combines **multiple predicates** (`or`, `not`, `exists`), a **result predicate** that mixes existence checks, and a **pass‑based end rule** (`passed both`). These constructs push the expressive limits of the grammar. |

Feel free to stop at any stage, or continue extending the game (e.g., adding a “trust‑bonus” effect, asymmetric boards, etc.). Enjoy playing **Honest Hare Of Trust**!

requests.response:
Below are **six incremental implementations** of the same game – **Accelerated Shiny Vulture** – each one adding a new rule that is a little harder to express in the Ludax language.  
All snippets are **stand‑alone, syntactically correct** Ludax programs (they can be fed to the compiler without any modification).

---

## 1️⃣  Minimal version – just place pieces, win by a line of three  

```lisp
(game "Accelerated Shiny Vulture – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))               ; 5 × 5 board
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; alternating turns
                (place
                    (destination empty) ; you may only place on empty squares
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))    ; three in a row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))     ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

*What we have*: a tiny abstract‑strategy game that is essentially “Tic‑Tac‑Toe on a 5×5 board”.  

---

## 2️⃣  Add a **start‑placement** – each player begins with a “shiny vulture” in opposite corners  

```lisp
(game "Accelerated Shiny Vulture – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))            ; P1’s vulture starts in the top‑left corner
            (place P2 (4 4))            ; P2’s vulture starts in the bottom‑right corner
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New element*: the **`start`** block lets us put pieces on the board before the first turn.  

---

## 3️⃣  Capture **adjacent opponent pieces** on every placement (and score them)  

```lisp
(game "Accelerated Shiny Vulture – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; --- capture any opponent piece that is orthogonally/diagonally
                    ;     adjacent to the newly placed piece
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))   ; mask = squares adjacent to opponent pieces
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed*:  
* `effect_capture` with mask **`(adjacent (occupied opponent))`** – every opponent piece that is next to the new piece is removed.  
* `increment_score:true` – the capturing player gets a point for each piece taken.  

---

## 4️⃣  Win by **score** (5 captured pieces) in addition to a line  

```lisp
(game "Accelerated Shiny Vulture – v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; three‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; 5 captured pieces wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New rule*: the predicate **`(>= (score mover) 5)`** checks the player’s accumulated capture score.  

---

## 5️⃣  **Force a pass** when a player has no legal move (i.e., no empty squares)  

```lisp
(game "Accelerated Shiny Vulture – v5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                               ; skip turn if no empty squares
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What `force_pass` does*: after the `place` mechanic the engine checks whether the mover can actually satisfy the destination constraint. If not, the turn is automatically passed to the opponent.  

---

## 6️⃣  **Accelerated win** – a line of **four** ends the game immediately (the “shiny vulture” can fly faster)  

```lisp
(game "Accelerated Shiny Vulture – v6 (final)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; four in a row → accelerated win
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; three still counts
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; capture‑score win
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Final touch*: the extra win condition **`(line 4)`** models the “accelerated” part of the game – once a player manages a line of four, the vulture’s speed is enough to claim victory instantly.

---

### How to use these snippets
1. **Copy** any version you like into a file with a `.ludax` extension.  
2. **Run** the Ludax compiler/interpreter on that file.  
3. Play the resulting game – the rules evolve exactly as shown from v1 → v6.

Enjoy experimenting with **Accelerated Shiny Vulture**!

Below are **six incremental implementations** of the same game – **“Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude”** – each one adding a new rule that is a little harder to express in the Ludax language.  
Every block is a **stand‑alone, compilable Ludax program** (you can paste any of them into a Ludax compiler and it will parse).

---

## 1️⃣  Version 1 – Pure piece‑count scoring  

*Goal*: Fill the 5 × 5 board. When the board is full the player with the most pieces wins.  

```lisp
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
;; Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude – v1
;; Simple “most pieces” win condition.
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))               ; 5 × 5 grid
    )

    (rules
        ;; No special start – the board begins empty.

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; alternating turns forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)   ; you may only put on an empty square
                    (effects
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover))) ; score = # of your pieces
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (passed both) (by_score)) ; when no moves left (full board) decide by score
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣ Version 2 – Capture adjacent opponent pieces  

*New rule*: When you place a piece, any orthogonal/diagonal neighbour that belongs to the opponent is **captured** (removed) and you gain an extra point for each capture.

```lisp
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
;; Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude – v2
;; Adds capture of adjacent opponent pieces (+1 point per capture).
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    (effects
                        ;; Capture every adjacent opponent piece.
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)

                        ;; Re‑calculate the total number of your pieces.
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (passed both) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣ Version 3 – Prescribed first move (center placement)  

*New rule*: Player 1 must start the game by placing a Hippo on the **center** square.  
The rest of the game proceeds as in v2.

```lisp
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
;; Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude – v3
;; First move forced to the centre of the board.
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            ;; 5 × 5 board → centre is cell 13 (0‑based row‑major order)
            (place P1 (13))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P2 P1)               ; after the forced start, P2 moves first
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (passed both) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

## 4️⃣ Version 4 – Forced‑pass when a player has no legal move  

*New rule*: If a player cannot place on any empty square (e.g., the board is already full or they are blocked by a rule), they **must pass**.  
The `force_pass` token tells the engine to treat “no legal destination” as a legal *pass* action.

```lisp

;; Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude – v4
;; Adds forced pass when a player has no legal destination.
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (13))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)               ; allow a pass if “empty” is unavailable
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (passed both) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

## 5️⃣ Version 5 – Line‑of‑4 win condition (plus score fallback)  

*New rule*: As soon as a player creates a **straight line of four** of their own Hippos (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) they **win immediately**.  
If the board fills without a line‑of‑4, the winner is decided by score as before.

## 6️⃣ Version 6 – Attempted **Amplitude‑based scoring** (illustrates a limitation)

The *flavour* of “Hippo Of Amplitude” is that each Hippo’s **score equals its Manhattan distance from the centre**; the player’s total score is the sum of those distances.  
Unfortunately the current Ludax grammar does **not provide a primitive for “distance from centre”** or for arbitrary arithmetic over board coordinates, so we cannot express this rule directly.

Below is a **best‑effort stub** that *records* the intended rule in a comment and falls back to the piece‑count scoring used in v5.  It shows how you would **document** an un‑expressible rule while keeping the program syntactically valid.

```lisp
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
;; Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude – v6
;; Desired: score = sum of Manhattan distances from centre.
;; Limitation: Ludax has no built‑in “distance‑from‑centre” function,
;; so we cannot compute the amplitude score directly.
;; The game therefore falls back to piece‑count scoring, but the
;; comment explains the intended mechanic for future extensions.
;; --------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Authentic Hippo Of Amplitude v6"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (13))                     ; centre start
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)

                        ;; Intended amplitude scoring (un‑expressible):
                        ;;   (set_score mover (sum_of_distances_from_center))
                        ;; Placeholder: keep simple piece count.
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

> **Take‑away:**  
> *Versions 1‑5 are fully expressible in Ludax.*  
> *Version 6 demonstrates a rule that **cannot** be encoded with the current grammar (the “amplitude” distance‑based scoring).*  
> If the language were extended with a function like `(manhattan_distance cell centre)` or a way to iterate over all pieces, the rule could be implemented directly.

## 3️⃣  Silky Thistle Seagull – Custodial capture  
*New rule:* when a player places a piece that **flanks** one or more opponent pieces (custodial capture), those opponent pieces are removed and the mover gains a point.  

```lisp
(game "Silky Thistle Seagull – Custodial Capture"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (48 48))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial any))   ; a capture is possible
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))   ; win by score as well
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Key points*  

* `mask_custodial` with argument `any` means “any length of flanked opponent pieces”.  
* `effect_capture` removes those pieces and, because of `increment_score:true`, adds 1 to the mover’s score.

## 4️⃣  Silky Thistle Seagull – Score‑based victory & forced pass  
*New rules:*  

1. **Score win** – a player who reaches **12 points** wins instantly.  
2. **Forced pass** – if a player has **no legal placement** (no empty square that would capture or create a line), they must pass. The game ends when **both** players pass consecutively, and the winner is decided by score.

```lisp
(game "Silky Thistle Seagull – Score & Forced Pass"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (48 48))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result
                        (or
                            (exists (custodial any))          ; capture possible
                            (exists (adjacent empty))         ; or just any empty neighbour
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)          ; if the above result fails, the player must pass
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 12) (mover win))          ; score win
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                       ; line win
            (if (passed both) (by_score))                   ; both passed → decide by score
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Explanation of the new constructs*  

* `play` now contains a **`result`** predicate that checks whether the move would either capture or at least be adjacent to an empty square – otherwise the move is illegal and the player must **pass** (`force_pass`).  
* The **end** clause adds a score‑based win (`>= 12`) and a “both passed” condition (`passed both`) that resolves the game **by score** (`by_score`).

## 1️⃣  Simple version – basic placement & capture  

**What we introduce**

* 8 × 8 square board.  
* Players alternately place a “finch” on any empty square.  
* Any orthogonal opponent piece that is **adjacent** to the newly placed finch is captured (removed) and the mover’s score is increased by 1.  
* Win when you have captured **5** pieces; otherwise a full board is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Delectable Horned Finch - Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Why this is “simple”* – Only a single **place** mechanic, a single **capture** effect and a trivial win‑condition. All of these map directly to the basic Ludax constructs.

---

## 2️⃣  Adding a “flip” (Reversi‑style) mechanic  

**What we add**

* After a placement the player may **flip** any opponent finches that are bounded by the new piece and another of the mover’s pieces in a straight line (the classic “custodial” pattern).  
* A move is **only legal** if it flips at least one opponent piece – expressed with a **result constraint**.

```lisp
(game "Delectable Horned Finch - Flip"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ;; The move must flip at least one opponent piece
                    (result (exists (custodial any)))

                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                        (flip (custodial any))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Why this is harder* –  
The **`result`** clause forces a predicate on the *post‑move* board state, and the **`flip`** effect uses the **custodial** mask, which is a more advanced mask type that the language must expand into multiple directional sub‑masks at compile time.

```lisp
                    )
```

*Why this is harder* – The **`start`** block introduces **initial placement patterns** that must be parsed before the first turn, a feature not used in the earlier examples.

**What we add**

*Board*: 5 × 5 square.

*Effect*: Every opponent piece that is **adjacent** to the newly placed piece is captured (removed) and the mover’s score is increased by 1.

```lisp
(game "Flat Ferret Of Blizzard – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
```

- `capture` → `(adjacent (occupied opponent))` captures all adjacent opponent stones.  
- `increment_score:true` automatically adds 1 per captured piece.  
- `line 3` is the classic “three‑in‑a‑row” win condition.

## 2️⃣  Adding **start‑placement** – each player begins with a “Ferret” in opposite corners  

The start phase lets us put a permanent piece on the board before normal play begins.

```lisp
(game "Flat Ferret Of Blizzard – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))          ; top‑left corner (index 0)
            (place P2 (24))         ; bottom‑right corner (index 24)
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we learned*:  
- The `start` block can contain any number of `place` statements.  
- `pattern_arg` is simply a list of board‑cell indices; here we used the two corner indices (0 and 24).

---

## 3️⃣  **Forced‑pass** rule – a player must pass when no legal move exists  

The previous version already contained a `force_pass` token, but let’s make the rule **explicit**: the token is placed *after* the `place` clause inside the repeat phase.

```lisp
(game "Flat Ferret Of Blizzard – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)               ; mandatory pass if no empty squares
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we learned*:  
- `force_pass` is a **stand‑alone** token that belongs to the same `play_mechanic` as the `place`.  
- When the mover cannot satisfy the `destination empty` constraint, the engine automatically treats the turn as a pass.

---

## 4️⃣  **Scoring win** – a player wins once they have captured 5 opponent pieces  

Now we add a second win condition based on the score that is built up by the capture effect.

```lisp
(game "Flat Ferret Of Blizzard – v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; three‑in‑a‑row
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; 5 captures
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we learned*:  
- The `score` function returns the current mover’s score.  
- Comparison predicates such as `>=` can be used directly in an `if` clause.

---

## 5️⃣  **Extra‑turn on a short line** – if a player creates a line of 2 they immediately get another move  

This rule is **harder to express** because Ludax’s `repeat` phase has a *fixed* mover order.  
To model “extra turn” we would need a conditional insertion of the mover into the order, which the current grammar does **not** support directly.

Below is a *partial* attempt that shows the idea, followed by a comment explaining why it cannot be compiled as‑is.

```lisp

    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )

        (play
            ;; The normal alternating order
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)

                ;; *** Desired extra‑turn logic (not supported) ***
                ;; If the mover just created a line of length 2, they should
                ;; play again before the opponent.
                ;; There is no construct like “if … then repeat mover”.
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

### Why it fails
* The grammar only allows a **static** list of movers in a `repeat` or `once_through` phase.  
* There is no predicate‑driven way to **inject** an extra mover into the sequence based on the result of the just‑made move.  
* To support this mechanic we would need an extension such as a “conditional repeat” block or a “repeat‑while” construct, which are not part of the current Ludax specification.

| v2 | Start‑placement of “Ferret” pieces | `start` block |

Below are **five incremental implementations** of the same abstract‑strategy game – **“Passionate Acoustic Muskox”** – written in the **ludax** language.  
Each step adds a new rule that is *harder to express* in ludax, while the previous version remains a **valid, compilable** ludax program.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – only “place on an empty square” and win by a line of 4  

```lisp
;; Passionate Acoustic Muskox – v1 (the simplest version)
(game "Passionate Acoustic Muskox v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))               ; 5 × 5 square board
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; alternating turns forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)  ; you may put a piece on any empty cell
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))    ; four in a row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))     ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

*What this version does:*  
- Two players (P1, P2) take turns placing a piece on any empty square.  
- The game ends when a player creates a line of four (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) or when the board is full.

---

## 2️⃣  Add **capture** of orthogonal‑adjacent opponent pieces  

```lisp
;; Passionate Acoustic Muskox – v2 (adds capture of adjacent opponent pieces)
(game "Passionate Acoustic Muskox v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        ;; Capture any orthogonal‑adjacent opponent piece(s)
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New rule:*  
- After placing, **all orthogonal neighbours that belong to the opponent are captured** (removed from the board).  
- `increment_score:true` automatically adds 1 to the mover’s score for each captured piece (the built‑in “capture” effect does that).

---

## 3️⃣  Keep a **running score** and update it after every move  

```lisp
;; Passionate Acoustic Muskox – v3 (adds explicit score tracking)
(game "Passionate Acoustic Muskox v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)

                        ;; Re‑calculate scores after the capture
                        (set_score mover   (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 8) (mover win))   ; first to 8 pieces wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed:*  
- After each move we **re‑set both players’ scores** to the number of pieces they currently own.  
- An extra win condition: a player who reaches **8 pieces** (i.e., a score of 8) wins immediately.

---

## 4️⃣  Allow a **forced pass** when a player has no legal move  

```lisp
;; Passionate Acoustic Muskox – v4 (adds forced pass)
(game "Passionate Acoustic Muskox v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover   (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                     ; if a player cannot place, they pass automatically
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 8) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))      ; both players passed → decide by score
        )
    )
)
```

*New mechanic:*  
- After each turn the engine checks whether the next player has **any empty square**.  
- If not, the `(force_pass)` clause makes that player automatically skip their turn.  
- When **both** players pass consecutively, the game ends and the winner is the player with the higher score (`by_score`).

---

## 5️⃣  Full‑featured version – start‑position, colour rendering, and all previous rules  

```lisp
;; Passionate Acoustic Muskox – v5 (complete version)
(game "Passionate Acoustic Muskox v5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; ---- Initial placement -------------------------------------------------
        (start
            ;; P1 starts in the exact centre of the 5×5 board (cell 13)
            (place P1 (13))
            ;; P2 starts in the four corners (cells 0, 4, 20, 24)
            (place P2 (0 4 20 24))
        )

        ;; ---- Main play loop ----------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)

                    (destination empty)

                )

            )

        ;; ---- End conditions ----------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; four in a row
            (if (>= (score mover) 8) (mover win))        ; or 8 pieces captured
            (if (full_board) (draw))                     ; board full → draw
            (if (passed both) (by_score))                ; both passed → decide by score
        )
    )

    ;; ---- Rendering (optional, but nice for a UI) -----------------------------
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

| **Start placement** – fixed centre for P1, corners for P2 | `(start … (place …))` |
| **Turn order** – alternating forever | `(repeat (P1 P2) …)` |

Below you will find **four incremental implementations** of the same game – *Lyrical Teal Of Variation* (LTV).  
Each step adds a new mechanic that is **harder to express in the Ludax language**, while every version remains a **stand‑alone, compilable Ludax program**.

---

## 1️⃣  LTV – Very Simple Version  

*Goal*: Get three of your pieces in a straight line (horizontal, vertical or diagonal).  
*Mechanics*: Players simply place a piece on any empty square; there is no start‑setup, no captures, no scoring.

```lisp
(game "Lyrical Teal Of Variation v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))          ; 5 × 5 board
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)        ; alternating turns forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)   ; can only put on empty squares
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))      ; three‑in‑a‑row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))       ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

*What was added later?*  
- A **start‑placement** phase.  
- **Capture** of adjacent opponent pieces.  
- **Scoring** and a **forced‑pass** rule.  
- A **win‑by‑score** end condition.

---

## 2️⃣  LTV – Start Placement  

Now each player begins with a single “teal” token placed in opposite corners.  
The rest of the game is unchanged.

```lisp
(game "Lyrical Teal Of Variation v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))          ; top‑left corner for P1
            (place P2 (24))         ; bottom‑right corner for P2
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What was added later?*  
- **Capture** of adjacent opponent pieces (requires a result‑constraint and an effect).  
- **Score‑keeping** after each move.  
- **Forced‑pass** when a player cannot move.  

---

## 3️⃣  LTV – Capture & Score Update  

When a piece is placed, any orthogonal or diagonal neighbour that belongs to the opponent is **captured** (removed) and the mover’s score is increased by 1 for each capture.  
We also keep the scores up‑to‑date after every move.

```lisp
(game "Lyrical Teal Of Variation v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; The move is only legal if it would capture at least one opponent piece
                    (result
                        (exists (adjacent occupied))
                    )

                    (effects
                        ; Capture every adjacent opponent piece and give the mover a point per capture
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) mover increment_score:true)

                        ; Refresh both players’ scores (useful for later win‑by‑score)
                        (set_score mover   (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What was added later?*  
- **Forced‑pass** when a player has no legal destination.  
- **Win‑by‑score** (first to 5 points).  

---

## 4️⃣  LTV – Full‑Featured Version  

All the previous mechanics are kept, plus:

1. **Forced pass** – if a player has **no empty square** that satisfies the destination mask, they automatically pass their turn.  
2. **Win‑by‑score** – a player wins as soon as they reach **5 points** (captures).  
3. **Draw‑by‑score** – if the board fills up and nobody has 5 points, the player with the higher score wins; a tie is a draw.  

```lisp
(game "Lyrical Teal Of Variation v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; Must capture at least one opponent piece; otherwise the move is illegal
                    (result
                        (exists (adjacent occupied))
                    )

                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) mover increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover     (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)          ; If the above placement is impossible, the player passes
            )
        )
        (end
            ; 1️⃣  Reach 5 points → immediate win
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))

            ; 2️⃣  Three‑in‑a‑row still wins (classic goal)
            (if (line 3) (mover win))

            ; 3️⃣  Full board → compare scores
            (if (full_board)
                (if (>= (score P1) (score P2))
                    (if (> (score P1) (score P2))
                        (P1 win)
                        (draw))
                    (P2 win))
            )
        )
    )
)
```

### How the “harder‑to‑express” parts are realised

| Feature | Why it is harder in Ludax | Ludax construct used |
|---------|---------------------------|----------------------|
| **Start placement** | Needs a separate phase before normal play. | `(start …)` block with `(place …)` |
| **Capture with score increment** | Must both remove opponent pieces *and* modify a numeric score. | `effect_capture` with `increment_score:true` + `set_score` |
| **Result constraint** | Disallow moves that would capture nothing. | `(result (exists (adjacent occupied)))` |
| **Forced pass** | Implicit “no‑legal‑move → skip turn”. | `(force_pass)` token after the `place` clause |
| **Win‑by‑score** | Numerical comparison against a threshold. | `if (>= (score mover) 5) …` |
| **Draw‑by‑score** | Nested conditional logic inside an `if`. | Nested `if` inside the `full_board` end‑rule |

---

You can now compile each of the four snippets with any Ludax interpreter.  
Start with version 1 to test the basic line‑win mechanic, then replace the source with version 2, 3, and finally 4 to see the richer rule‑set come alive. Enjoy playing *Lyrical Teal Of Variation*!

Invent simple rules for a novel two player abstract strategy game called Wapiti Of Pastoral Advance. Implement it in the ludax language.         grammar.lark:
// ---Root---
game: "(" "game" name players equipment rules rendering? ")"

// ---Players---
players: "(" "players" positive_int ")"

// ---Equipment---
equipment: "(" "equipment" board ")"
board: "(" "board" (board_square | board_rectangle | board_hexagon | board_hex_rectangle) ")"
board_square: "(" "square" number ")"
board_rectangle: "(" "rectangle" number number ")"
board_hexagon: "(" "hexagon"  number ")"
board_hex_rectangle: "(" "hex_rectangle" number number ")"

// ---Rules---
rules: "(" "rules" start_rules? play_rules end_rules ")"

// ---Start rules---
start_rules: "(" "start" start_rule+ ")"
start_rule: start_place
start_place: "(" "place" player_reference pattern_arg ")"

// ---Play rules---
play_rules: "(" "play" play_phase+ ")"
play_phase: phase_once_through | phase_repeat
phase_once_through: "(" "once_through" play_mover_order play_mechanic ")"
phase_repeat: "(" "repeat" play_mover_order play_mechanic ")"
play_mover_order: "(" player_reference+ ")"

play_mechanic: play_place force_pass? 
play_place: "(" "place" mover_reference? place_destination_constraint place_result_constraint? play_effects?")"
force_pass: "(" "force_pass" ")"

// ---Constraints---
place_destination_constraint: "(" "destination" super_mask ")"
place_result_constraint: "(" "result" super_predicate ")"

// ---Effects---
play_effects: "(" "effects" play_effect+ ")"
play_effect: effect_capture 
           | effect_flip
           | effect_increment_score
           | effect_set_score

effect_capture: "(" "capture" super_mask mover_reference? increment_score_arg? ")"
effect_flip: "(" "flip" super_mask mover_reference? ")"
effect_increment_score: "(" "increment_score" mover_reference function ")"
effect_set_score: "(" "set_score" mover_reference function ")"

// ---Functions---
function: function_add
        | function_connected
        | function_constant
        | function_count
        | function_line
        | function_multiply
        | function_score
        | function_subtract

function_add: "(" "add" function+ ")"
function_connected: "(" "connected" multi_mask_arg mover_reference? direction_arg? ")"
function_constant: positive_int
function_count: "(" "count" super_mask ")"
function_line: "(" "line" positive_int orientation_arg? exact_arg? ")"
function_multiply: "(" "multiply" function+ ")"
function_score: "(" "score" mover_reference ")"
function_subtract: "(" "subtract" function function ")"

// ---End rules---
end_rules: "(" "end" end_rule+ ")"
end_rule: "(" "if" super_predicate end_rule_result ")"
?end_rule_result: result_win | result_lose | result_draw | result_by_score

// -- Result definitions --
result_win: "(" mover_reference "win" ")"
result_lose: "(" mover_reference "lose" ")"
result_draw: "(" "draw" ")"
result_by_score: "(" "by_score" ")"

// -- Mask definitions --
super_mask: mask | super_mask_and | super_mask_or | super_mask_not
super_mask_and: "(" "and" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_or: "(" "or" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_not: "(" "not" super_mask ")"

mask: mask_adjacent
    | mask_center
    | mask_corners
    | mask_custodial
    | mask_edge
    | mask_empty
    | mask_occupied
    | mask_pattern
    | mask_prev_move

mask_adjacent: "(" "adjacent" super_mask direction_arg? ")"
mask_center: "center"
mask_corners: "corners"
mask_custodial: "(" "custodial" custodial_length_arg mover_reference? orientation_arg? ")"
mask_edge: "(" "edge" edge ")"
mask_empty: "empty"
mask_occupied: "occupied" | "(" "occupied" mover_reference ")"
mask_pattern: "(" "pattern" dimensions_arg pattern_arg rotate_arg? ")"
mask_prev_move: "(" "prev_move" mover_reference ")"

// "Multi-masks" are special keywords that are manually split into multiple
// sub-masks at compile time. This is mostly useful for the "connected" function,
// which expects a list of masks to check for connections between
multi_mask: multi_mask_corners
          | multi_mask_edges
          | multi_mask_edges_no_corners

multi_mask_corners: "corners"
multi_mask_edges: "edges"
multi_mask_edges_no_corners: "edgesNoCorners"

// ---Predicate definitions---
super_predicate: predicate | super_predicate_and | super_predicate_or | super_predicate_not
super_predicate_and: "(" "and" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_or: "(" "or" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_not: "(" "not" super_predicate ")"

predicate: predicate_equals
         | predicate_exists
         | predicate_full_board
         | predicate_function
         | predicate_greater_equals
         | predicate_less_equals
         | predicate_mover_is
         | predicate_passed

predicate_equals: "(" "=" function+ ")"
predicate_exists: "(" "exists" super_mask ")" // technically equivalent to (>= (count mask) 1) 
predicate_full_board: "(" "full_board" ")"
predicate_function: function // special syntax which is equivalent to "(>= function 1)"
predicate_greater_equals: "(" ">=" function function ")"
predicate_less_equals: "(" "<=" function function ")"
predicate_mover_is: "(" "mover_is" player_reference ")"
predicate_passed: "(" "passed" (mover_reference | BOTH) ")"

// Additional (potentially optional) arguments for predicates
custodial_length_arg: ANY | positive_int
dimensions_arg: "(" positive_int positive_int ")"
direction_arg: "direction:" direction
exact_arg: "exact:" boolean
increment_score_arg: "increment_score:" boolean
multi_mask_arg: multi_mask | "(" super_mask+ ")"
orientation_arg: "orientation:" orientation
pattern_arg: "(" positive_int+ ")"
rotate_arg: "rotate:" boolean

// Optional rendering details
rendering: "(" "rendering" rendering_detail+ ")"
rendering_detail: color_assignment

color_assignment: "(" "color" player_reference color ")"

// General-purpose definitions
?number: SIGNED_NUMBER
?positive_int: /[0-9]+/
?boolean: TRUE | FALSE
?edge: TOP | BOTTOM | LEFT | RIGHT | TOP_LEFT | TOP_RIGHT | BOTTOM_LEFT | BOTTOM_RIGHT
?direction: UP | DOWN | LEFT | RIGHT | UP_LEFT | UP_RIGHT | DOWN_LEFT | DOWN_RIGHT | VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?orientation: VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?color: WHITE | BLACK
// ----------------------------

?player_reference: P1 | P2
?mover_reference: MOVER | OPPONENT
name: STRING
variable_name: /\?[a-z][a-z0-9]*/
id: /[a-zA-Z0-9_]+/

// Constants
TOP: "top"
BOTTOM: "bottom"
UP: "up"
DOWN: "down"
LEFT: "left"
RIGHT: "right"
TOP_LEFT: "top_left"
TOP_RIGHT: "top_right"
BOTTOM_LEFT: "bottom_left"
BOTTOM_RIGHT: "bottom_right"
UP_LEFT: "up_left"
UP_RIGHT: "up_right"
DOWN_LEFT: "down_left"
DOWN_RIGHT: "down_right"
VERTICAL: "vertical"
HORIZONTAL: "horizontal"
ORTHOGONAL: "orthogonal"
DIAGONAL: "diagonal"
BACK_DIAGONAL: "back_diagonal"
FORWARD_DIAGONAL: "forward_diagonal"
ANY: "any"
TRUE: "true"
FALSE: "false"
MOVER: "mover"
OPPONENT: "opponent"
P1: "P1"
P2: "P2"
BOTH: "both"
WHITE: "white"
BLACK: "black"

// Lark imports
%import common.ESCAPED_STRING -> STRING
%import common.SIGNED_NUMBER
%import common.WS
%ignore WS


        example games:
        

(game "Complexity Demo" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 10))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent (adjacent (prev_move opponent))))))
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move mover)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Four"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 6 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination (and empty (or (edge bottom) (adjacent occupied direction:up)))))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Six"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 6) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Hex"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hex_rectangle 11 11))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge top) (edge bottom))) 2) (mover_is P1)) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge left) (edge right))) 2) (mover_is P2)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Gomoku" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 15))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5 exact:true) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Pente" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination center))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Reversi" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (28 35))
            (place P2 (27 36))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (exists
                            (custodial any)
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (flip (custodial any))
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end
            (if (passed both) (by_score))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)


(game "Tic-Tac-Toe" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 3))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalath" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hexagon 9))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play 
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (line 3) (mover lose))
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalax" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 13))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (and 
                            (not (line 5))
                            (not (= (line 4) 1))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (>= (line 4) 2) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
